anchor: Realtor teams are a WhatsApp-agent goldmine

posts.body_preview:
A Toronto realtor team was losing ~40% of inbound leads overnight. Not because of the leads. Because of the agents (the human ones).

I shipped them a WhatsApp agent. Here's what happened in 60 days 👇
My solo-founder ops stack, April 2026. I run a services agency, a product (UpliftAI), and a catering business. Here's exactly what my week looks like — scheduled to the 30-minute block.
Tip: name your agents. Seriously. 'Houseup's intake bot' is forgettable. 'Maya' is not. Users trust things with names. Even when they know it's software.
The stack we use at UpliftAI has 4 layers. Most agencies are only building layer 2. That's why their agents feel dumb.

Which part of your business would you automate first if you had zero skill limits?

I'm collecting answers for a playbook.
Catered a 60-person wedding tonight. Agent took the booking, auto-generated the invoice, and sent the delivery confirmation. First time I've run an event without touching email once.
Toronto realtor team was losing 40% of inbound leads overnight. I shipped a WhatsApp agent and the funnel flipped. Watch the bot qualify a lead in real time.

I built a WhatsApp agent for a Toronto realtor team (full 22-minute walkthrough). Screen recording of every flow — qualification, showing booking, objection handling. Stack + prompts in the description.
